name: High-Priority Function Calling Champions
description: Test the top BFCL v4 performers that fit on Jetson. These models scored higher than anything we've tested.

models.why: 51.4 BFCL at 3B = potentially faster AND smarter for tool calling than our current 4B default
41.22 BFCL, 87.5% parallel FC at only 3B. Salesforce FC specialist.
46.68 BFCL with 87.5% parallel FC. Best 8B tool caller.

name: Alternative 8B Models — Head-to-Head
description: Compare the best 8B models across different specializations to validate our Qwen3.5 choice.

models.why: 46.23 BFCL with 93.12% Multiple FC — highest of ANY model on BFCL
42.44 BFCL, 87.1% simple FC. Huawei/USTC tool specialist.
42.57 BFCL, 93.75% parallel FC. GGUF already on device.

name: Ultra-Lightweight Speed Demons
description: Test tiny models for instant hot-swap routing layer and latency-critical tool calls.

models.why: 30.44 BFCL at 1B. ~39 tok/s predicted. Tool routing layer candidate.
28.41 BFCL at 1.7B. Thinking mode available. ~23.5 tok/s.
18.98 BFCL at 350M. ~117 tok/s. Instant routing classifier.

name: Tight-Fit Deep Reasoners
description: Push the 8GB boundary with larger models at aggressive quantization. May use swap.

models.why: 41.03 BFCL at 14B. Deeper reasoning. ~6GB tight fit.
models.blocker: May exceed RAM, swap performance unknown

models.why: 30.43 BFCL, best multi-turn (27.53%) among viable models. Multimodal capable.
models.blocker: Tight fit, Q3 quality loss unknown

models.why: 28.79 BFCL, 24.73% multi-turn. MIT license. Microsoft architecture.
models.blocker: 14B at Q3 = quality vs size tradeoff

name: Custom Role Benchmarks
description: Run our 30-task role benchmark suite across all tested models. Grade outputs scientifically. Produce the definitive Jetson role-model mapping.
